encounter.reason: evaluation of persistent cough, unintentional weight loss, and right upper lobe opacity; CT chest review

conditions.name: suspected primary lung neoplasm of right upper lobe
conditions.note: 4.2 cm spiculated mass with ipsilateral mediastinal lymphadenopathy (stations 4R and 7), highly suspicious for non-small cell lung cancer. Tissue diagnosis not yet obtained. Radiographic staging suggests at least N2 disease.

conditions.name: persistent cough, nonproductive
conditions.note: 10 weeks duration, not responsive to antihistamines or benzonatate. Assessed as likely secondary to right upper lobe mass.

conditions.name: unintentional weight loss
conditions.note: Approximately 8 pounds over 2-3 months. Assessed as concerning for malignancy-related cachexia.

conditions.name: essential hypertension
conditions.note: Well-controlled on lisinopril 10 mg daily. No change in management.

document_references.description: Initial pulmonary consultation documenting review of CT chest with contrast findings (4.2 cm spiculated right upper lobe mass with mediastinal lymphadenopathy), clinical assessment of high suspicion for primary lung malignancy, detailed physical examination findings including diminished right apical breath sounds, plan for pulmonary function testing and urgent referral to MD Anderson Cancer Center for staging workup and tissue diagnosis. Includes discussion with patient and husband regarding findings and next steps.

diagnostic_reports.description: Imaging performed 2024-08-08 at an external facility; reviewed and interpreted by Dr. Ramaswamy at this encounter. Findings: 4.2 cm spiculated mass in the right upper lobe; ipsilateral mediastinal lymphadenopathy at station 4R (largest node 2.1 cm) and station 7 (1.4 cm); no pleural effusion; no contralateral mediastinal or hilar lymphadenopathy; no osseous abnormalities. Interpreted as highly suspicious for primary lung malignancy.
Performed 2024-08-01 at referring office (Westheimer Family Medicine), reviewed at this encounter. Hemoglobin 11.8 g/dL noted as mildly low; otherwise described as unremarkable.
Performed 2024-08-01 at referring office (Westheimer Family Medicine), reviewed at this encounter. Albumin 3.4 g/dL noted as mildly low; remainder unremarkable.
